question: Choose the antonym of the given word: Brutal
explanation: The antonym of 'brutal' (which means cruel or barbarous) will be 'gentle'. Hence option (a) would be correct.  /  The antonym of 'brutal' (which means cruel or barbarous) will be 'gentle'. Hence option (a) is correct.

question: Choose the synonym of the given word: Deceptive
explanation: The synonym of 'Deceptive' will be 'Misleading'. Hence option (d) would be correct.  /  The synonym of 'Deceptive' is 'Misleading'. Hence option (d) is correct.

question: Give one word for the given definition: One who writes stories and news for a newspaper.
explanation: A correspondent's job is to write stories and news for a newspaper or agency.

question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom: A bird of passage
explanation: A bird of passage means a person who travels widely. Hence option (c) is correct.

question: Choose the correct adjective of the following noun: Advice
explanation: 'Advice' is a proposal for a course of action. So 'Advisable' would be the correct adjective as it means worthy of being recommended.  /  'Advice' is a proposal for a course of action. 'Advisable' is the correct adjective meaning worthy of being recommended.

question: Complete the following sentence: Will you ……. a car, if you …… any money?
explanation: The correct verbs are: Will you buy a car, if you don't have any money.

question: Complete the sentence: If the result of the admission test ………. today, she …….. a telegram.
explanation: Since the preposition is in present tense, the correct sentence would be: If the result is announced today, she will send a telegram.  /  Since the preposition is in present tense: If the result is announced today, she will send a telegram.

question: Complete the following sentence by choosing the appropriate verb forms: Mr. Indrajeet Gupta ……… that he ………. truth to those people.  /  Complete the following sentence: Mr. Indrajeet Gupta ……… that he ………. truth to those people.
explanation: Claimed, spoke will be filled in the given fill in the blanks because there are two clauses in the sentence and both should be in same tense.  /  Both clauses should be in same tense. 'Claimed, spoke' is correct.

question: Point out the correct sentence of the following.

options.A: He and I was very good friends once upon a time
options.B: The committee were divided in their decision
options.C: Every student should do one's duty
options.D: Each boy and each girl have done their work

explanation: 'Committee', being plural, would be followed by 'were'. The correct sentence is: The committee were divided in their decision.  /  'Committee' being plural is followed by 'were'. The correct sentence is: 'The committee were divided in their decision.'

question: Choose the correct synonym of the word 'Exile'
explanation: 'Exile' means to banish someone out of a place. Hence option (b) Banishment would be correct.  /  'Exile' means to banish someone from a place. Hence Banishment is correct.

question: Choose the correct word: We heard ……….. of trumpets.
explanation: 'Peal' means a loud sound of trumpets. Hence option (a) would be correct.  /  'Peal' means a loud sound of trumpets. Hence option (a) is correct.

question: Point out the figure of speech: 'The quality of mercy is not strained. It droppeth as the gentle rain from heaven.'
explanation: Since 'as' is used the above sentence is an example of simile.  /  Since 'as' is used, the above sentence is an example of Simile.

question: Fill the appropriate set of words: 'The bridal …… of my daughter will be …… on 10th December.'
explanation: The word 'Ceremony' always follows bridal. The bridal ceremony of my daughter will be performed on 10th December.  /  The word 'Ceremony' always follows bridal. Correct sentence: 'The bridal ceremony of my daughter will be performed on 10th December.'

question: Fill in the blanks with the correct preposition: 'She went ……… the park ……… a walk.'
explanation: She went to the park for a walk is the correct sentence.

question: Name the poet who wrote the poem 'Stopping by the woods on a snowy evening.'
explanation: 'Stopping by the woods on a snowy evening' is a poem by Robert Frost.

question: Vocabulary can be taught by using the following technique
explanation: Vocabulary can be taught by using all the above mentioned techniques.  /  Vocabulary can be taught by using all the above-mentioned techniques.

question: Who said, 'Reading makes a full man, writing an exact man and conference a ready man'?
explanation: Above lines have been taken from Francis Bacon's essay 'of studies'.  /  These lines are from Francis Bacon's essay 'Of Studies'.

question: Which one is regarded as the best style of writing?
explanation: Vertical style of writing scripts is the best.  /  Vertical style of writing is considered the best.

question: Structural grammar emphasize  /  Structural grammar emphasizes
explanation: Structural grammar emphasizes on the words, phrases and structure of sentences.  /  Structural grammar emphasizes words, phrases and structure of sentences.

question: Which method is based on the theory 'from example to generalization'?
explanation: The Induction method is based on the theory of 'specific example to generalization.'  /  The Inductive method is based on the theory of 'specific example to generalization.'

question: 'Julius Ceaser' was written by  /  'Julius Caesar' was written by
explanation: 'Julius Ceaser' is a tragedy by William Shakespeare.  /  'Julius Caesar' is a tragedy by William Shakespeare.

question: Choose the correct sentence.

options.A: You should either come today or tomorrow.
options.B: I had scarcely reached home when it started raining heavily.
options.C: I had no sooner finished my studies when I got a job.

explanation: In sentence (a), 'tomorrow' is wrong and 'day after' should be used. In sentence (c), 'than' should be used. In sentence (d), 'I' should be used. Sentence (b) is correct.  /  Sentence (b) is correct: 'I had scarcely reached home when it started raining heavily.'

question: Fill the correct articles: ……….Sun rises in ……….East.
explanation: The correct sentence will be: The sun rises in the East. 'The' is a definite article which is the case here.  /  The correct sentence is: 'The sun rises in the East.' 'The' is a definite article used here.

question: Find the meaning of the following idiom: To read between the lines

options.C: to understand the hidden meaning of the word

explanation: 'To read between the lines' means to understand the hidden meaning of the word.  /  'To read between the lines' means to understand the hidden meaning.

question: Identify the underlined part of speech: 'The burglar disappeared with the cash.'  /  Identify the underlined part of speech: 'The burglar disappeared with the cash.' ('disappeared' is underlined)
explanation: Intransitive verbs do not allow an object to follow directly. 'Disappeared' is an intransitive verb as no object follows it.  /  Intransitive verbs do not take a direct object. 'Disappeared' is intransitive.

question: Pick out the incorrectly spelt word.
explanation: 'Tradgedy' is wrong and 'tragedy' would be correct.  /  'Tradgedy' is incorrectly spelt; the correct spelling is 'tragedy'.

question: Find out the grammatically wrong sentence

options.A: She takes her dog out for a walk
options.C: She go to the market for vegetables

explanation: In sentence (c) 'go' should be replaced with 'goes'. Hence option (c) would be correct.  /  'She go' is grammatically wrong; it should be 'She goes'.

question: Point out the Active Voice: 'The patients are visited by their relatives only between 4 to 6 pm'

options.A: Only between 4 to 6 pm the patients are visited by the relatives
options.B: The relatives visit the patients only between 4 to 6 pm
options.C: The relatives visited the patients only between 4 to 6 pm
options.D: Only between 4 to 6 pm the relatives can visit the patients

explanation: Since 'are' is used in the passive, the active voice will be in present tense. The correct active sentence: The relatives visit the patients only between 4 to 6 pm.  /  Active voice: The relatives visit the patients only between 4 to 6 pm.

question: Point out the sentence in Passive Voice.

options.A: The striking students were throwing stones
options.D: The enemy will have sunk the ship by now

explanation: 'have been' used in the second sentence hints that it is passive voice.  /  'Twenty lessons have been finished' uses 'have been' indicating passive voice.

question: Choose the correct option: 'The Prime Minister was acquitted ……….. the charge levelled against him.'
explanation: 'acquitted' is followed by 'of'. Hence option (a) would be correct.  /  'Acquitted' is followed by 'of'. Hence option (a) is correct.

question: The line 'A thing of beauty is a joy forever' has been written by which of the following?
explanation: The line quoted is from the poem 'Endymion' composed by the great romantic poet John Keats.  /  The line 'A thing of beauty is a joy forever' is from the poem 'Endymion' by John Keats.

question: Fill in the blank with the correct phrasal verb: It isn't easy to ........... children now-a-days.
explanation: The Hindi meaning of the sentence is 'आजकल बच्चों का लालन-पालन आसान नहीं।' Bring up means to raise/nurture children.  /  'Bring up' means to raise/nurture children. 'It isn't easy to bring up children nowadays.'

question: Pick out the comparative form of the sentence: He is as wise as Solomon

options.A: Soloman is wiser than he is
options.B: Soloman is not wiser than he is
options.D: Soloman is the wisest among them

explanation: Positive degree affirmative converts to Comparative negative. 'He is as wise as Solomon' → 'Solomon is not wiser than he is.'  /  Converting positive degree to comparative: 'He is as wise as Solomon' → 'Solomon is not wiser than he is.'

question: Point out the figure of speech: 'Here's the smell of the blood still, all the perfumes of Arabia will not sweeten this little hand'.
explanation: When any event is greatly exaggerated or understated, it is called Hyperbole. This line is from Macbeth and represents extreme exaggeration.  /  This sentence is an example of Hyperbole — it greatly exaggerates the extent of guilt. From Shakespeare's Macbeth.

question: Who wrote 'Some books are to be tasted, others to be swallowed and some few to be chewed and digested'?
explanation: Francis Bacon wrote these words in his essay 'Of Studies', advising readers how different books should be read with different levels of attention.  /  Francis Bacon wrote this in his essay 'Of Studies', advising how to read different kinds of books.

question: 'Shylock' is a character in which play?
explanation: Shakespeare's play 'The Merchant of Venice' features Shylock as the villainous moneylender — extremely cruel and greedy.  /  Shylock is a character in Shakespeare's play 'The Merchant of Venice'.

question: Complete the following proverb: The pen is mightier …….. .
explanation: The complete proverb is 'The pen is mightier than the sword', meaning writing/ideas are more powerful than physical force.  /  The complete proverb is: 'The pen is mightier than the sword.'

question: Fill in the blank with the correct preposition: He killed two birds.........one stone.
explanation: When a task is accomplished using a tool/instrument, 'With' is used as preposition. Correct sentence: He killed two birds with one stone.  /  Correct: 'He killed two birds with one stone.' 'With' is used when an instrument/tool is involved.

question: 'Arms and the Man' was written by which playwright?
explanation: 'Arms and the Man' is a world-class play by George Bernard Shaw, who received the Nobel Prize for Literature.  /  'Arms and the Man' is a play by George Bernard Shaw, Nobel Prize winner for Literature.

question: Point out the active voice of the following sentence: 'This notice has been altered.'  /  Point out the active voice: 'This notice has been altered.'

options.A: Alteration has been made in this notice by someone
options.C: Already the notice has been altered
options.D: The notice had been altered previously

explanation: In Passive to Active conversion, the Object of Passive becomes Subject of Active. Since no object is present, 'Someone' is used. Correct: 'Someone has altered this notice.'  /  Active voice of 'This notice has been altered' is 'Someone has altered this notice.'

question: Fill in the blank using the correct prepositional phrase: Shall we play indoors ............. sweating out in the sun  /  Fill in the blank: Shall we play indoors ............. sweating out in the sun
explanation: The Hindi meaning is 'क्या हमें खेलना चाहिए भीतर बजाय सूर्य की गर्मी में पसीना बहाने के।' 'Instead of' is correct here.  /  Correct: 'Shall we play indoors instead of sweating out in the sun.'

question: Fill in the blank with an appropriate choice: If you make a promise, you must be sun to ......... it.  /  Fill in the blank: If you make a promise, you must be sun to ......... it.
explanation: 'Keep' means to fulfil a promise. Correct: 'If you make a promise, you must be sun to keep it.'  /  Correct: 'If you make a promise, you must be sure to keep it.' 'Keep' means to fulfil.

question: Choose the incorrect part of the sentence: 'One of Mohan's ambitions (a)/ in life were to visit (b)/ all the historic places (c)/ in the country.(d)'  /  Choose the incorrect part: 'One of Mohan's ambitions (a)/ in life were to visit (b)/ all the historic places (c)/ in the country.(d)'
explanation: Part (b) has an error. 'One of Mohan's ambitions' refers to a Singular Noun, so 'was' should be used instead of 'were'. Correct: 'One of Mohan's ambitions in life was to visit all the historic places in the country.'  /  Error is in (b): 'were' should be 'was'. Correct: 'One of Mohan's ambitions in life was to visit...'

question: Choose the correct part of speech of the underlined word: It is a unique book.
explanation: Words that qualify Nouns are Adjectives. Here 'book' is a Noun and 'unique' describes it. Hence 'unique' is an Adjective.  /  'Unique' qualifies the noun 'book', so it is an Adjective.

question: Transform the following sentence by using 'too': She is over anxious for the safety of her son.  /  Transform using 'too': She is over anxious for the safety of her son.

options.A: She is too anxious that she wants the safety of her son
options.B: She is too anxious for the safety of her son
options.C: She is too anxious that her son may be safe
options.D: She is too anxious so that her son might be safe

explanation: 'Over' is synonymous with 'too'. The correct transformation is: 'She is too anxious for the safety of her son.'  /  Since 'over' = 'too', the correct transformation is: 'She is too anxious for the safety of her son.'

question: Choose the word opposite in meaning to: 'Atheist'  /  Choose the word opposite in meaning to 'Atheist'
explanation: Atheist means one who does not believe in God. The appropriate opposite is 'Believer' — one who believes in God.  /  Atheist = one who doesn't believe in God. Opposite = 'Believer' (one who believes in God).

question: Choose the word similar in meaning: Industrious  /  Choose the word similar in meaning to 'Industrious'
explanation: Industrious means hardworking. 'Diligent' is a synonym meaning hardworking/meticulous.  /  Industrious means hardworking. Synonym: 'Diligent' (hardworking, careful in work).

question: Change into indirect speech: Our teacher said to us, 'Sugar dissolves in water.'

options.A: Our teacher told us that sugar dissolved in water
options.B: Our teacher told us that sugar has dissolved in water
options.C: Our teacher said to us that sugar dissolves in water
options.D: Our teacher told us that sugar dissolves in water

explanation: The sentence is a Universal Truth, so no tense change is required. The correct indirect speech is: 'Our teacher told us that sugar dissolves in water.'  /  Universal truths don't change tense in indirect speech. Correct: 'Our teacher told us that sugar dissolves in water.'

question: Choose the appropriate synonym of word: Opulent  /  Choose the appropriate synonym of 'Opulent'
explanation: Opulent means extremely wealthy/rich. The synonym 'Wealthy' is correct.  /  Opulent means extremely wealthy. Synonym: 'Wealthy'.

question: Write the name of the writer of the lesson 'A Fellow-Traveler'.
explanation: 'A Fellow-Traveler' is an entertaining story in which the writer and a mosquito travel alone in a train compartment. The author is A. G. Gardiner.  /  'A Fellow-Traveler' was written by A. G. Gardiner — a story about a writer and a mosquito in a train compartment.

question: Fill in the blanks with the correct preposition: She has been living .......... this city .......... ten year.  /  Fill in the blanks: She has been living .......... this city .......... ten years.
explanation: Hindi: 'वह रह रही है इस शहर में दस साल से।' 'In' is used for the city and 'for' is used with Perfect continuous tense for duration. Correct: 'She has been living in this city for ten years.'  /  Correct: 'She has been living in this city for ten years.' 'In' for place; 'for' for duration.

question: Choose the opposite of the word: Masticate  /  Choose the opposite of 'Masticate'
explanation: Masticate means to chew slowly. The opposite is 'Gobble' — to swallow hastily.  /  Masticate = chew slowly. Opposite = Gobble (swallow hastily without chewing).

question: Select the antonym of underlined word: This frivolous remarks provoked no comments.  /  Select the antonym of 'frivolous': 'This frivolous remarks provoked no comments.'
explanation: Frivolous means not serious/silly. The antonym is 'Serious' — meaning important and thoughtful.  /  Frivolous = not serious. Antonym = 'Serious'.

question: Pedagogical knowledge includes
explanation: Pedagogical knowledge means knowledge of teaching methods and subject content. Both (a) and (b) are correct.  /  Pedagogical knowledge includes knowledge of both content and teaching method.

question: What is the skill among the ones given below that cannot be tested in a formal written examination?  /  Which skill cannot be tested in a formal written examination?
explanation: A formal written examination cannot assess whether a student reads extensively for pleasure, as this is an intrinsic motivation that cannot be evaluated through a formal test.  /  Extensive reading for pleasure cannot be tested in a formal written examination — it is intrinsically motivated.

question: Drills are considered important in which method?
explanation: Drills mean practice exercises, used in the Pattern Practice method for language learning.  /  Drills are important in the Pattern Practice method for language learning.

question: Which of the following is a lexical word?
explanation: Lexical words are content words found in the dictionary. 'Principal' is a lexical word; 'if', 'some', and 'whether' are grammatical function words.  /  'Principal' is a lexical (content) word. 'If', 'Some', and 'Whether' are function words.

question: English is a ........... language in India.
explanation: English is a second language in India ('दूसरी भाषा').  /  English is a second language in India.

question: Select the appropriate alternative which best expresses the meaning: To beat the air  /  Select the meaning: To beat the air

options.B: To make effort that are useless or vain

explanation: To beat the air means to make efforts that are useless or vain — to do something that produces no result.  /  'To beat the air' means to make useless or vain efforts — to accomplish nothing.

question: Select the appropriate synonym of word TRUNCATE.  /  Select the appropriate synonym of TRUNCATE.
explanation: Truncate means to cut or shorten. The synonym is 'Cut off', expressed by the Phrasal Verb 'Cut off'.  /  Truncate means to cut short. Synonym: 'Cut off'.

question: Sardar Patel ........... the farmers of Bardoli in their fight against the government.  /  Fill in the blank: Sardar Patel ........... the farmers of Bardoli in their fight against the government.
explanation: दिये गये वाक्य के रिक्त स्थान में past tense का word 'led – influenced or organised by' उपयुक्त होगा, क्योंकि वाक्य में बीती घटनाओं के बारे में चर्चा हो रही है। Note: Led – V IIIrd form of 'lead'.  /  'Led' is the past tense of 'lead', meaning organised/influenced. The sentence refers to past events.

question: Choose the masculine gender from the options given below.
explanation: Drake नर बतख (female – duck) है, So it is a masculine gender word. Roc – हिरनी, Countess – अमीर स्त्री, Peahen – मोरनी।  /  Drake is the masculine gender of duck. Roe=female deer, Countess=female noble, Peahen=female peacock.

question: Which of the following sentences has a request?

options.B: Could I borrow some money from you, please?
options.C: Wish you a happy journey together.

explanation: Option (b) में please शब्द का प्रयोग हुआ है जो request भाव प्रकट करता है। अन्य options में requesting word का use नहीं किया गया है। अतः correct sentence: Could I borrow some money from you, please?  /  'Could I borrow some money from you, please?' contains 'please' which makes it a request.

question: Which of the following word does not have silent letter/letters?
explanation: Silent letter – Silent letter वह अक्षर होते हैं, जो शब्द में तो होते हैं परन्तु pronunciation (उच्चारण) में छिप जाते हैं। जैसे: Psychology में 'P', Doubt में 'b' व Knowledge में 'k' silent है। Cattle के उच्चारण में सभी अक्षरों का प्रयोग हो रहा है।  /  'Cattle' has no silent letter. Psychology(P silent), Doubt(b silent), Knowledge(k silent).

question: Which conjunction will be the most appropriate to join the following sentences? You will not succeed. You work harder.  /  Which conjunction joins: 'You will not succeed. You work harder.'
explanation: Unless – जब तक कि नहीं। इसका प्रयोग condition के तौर पर negative sense में किया जाता है। अतः इसके साथ वाले sentence में no या not का प्रयोग नहीं करते हैं। अतः correct sentence: You will not succeed unless you work hard.  /  'Unless' is correct: 'You will not succeed unless you work harder.' Unless introduces a negative condition.

question: Which of the following sentences has been punctuated correctly?

options.A: Renu said, "Give me a pen, a pencil and a book."
options.B: Renu said Give me a pen, a pencil and a book.
options.C: Renu said give me a pen a pencil and a book.
options.D: Renu said "Give me a pen a pencil and a book."

explanation: Correct punctuated sentence: Renu said, "Give me a pen, a pencil and a book."  /  Correct: Renu said, "Give me a pen, a pencil and a book." – with comma after reporting verb and quoted speech in inverted commas.

question: Fill in the blank in the following sentence with the correct alternative: I did not inform ............... at all.  /  Fill in the blank: I did not inform ............... at all.
explanation: दिये गये वाक्य में रिक्त स्थान में anybody का प्रयोग होगा क्योंकि negative वाक्य में anybody का ही प्रयोग होता है। So, the correct sentence is: I did not inform anybody at all.  /  'Anybody' is used in negative sentences. Correct: 'I did not inform anybody at all.'

question: The antonym of 'Justice' is
explanation: Justice – न्याय, का Appropriate antonym शब्द Injustice – अन्याय होगा।  /  The antonym of 'Justice' is 'Injustice'.

question: The synonym of 'Adroit' is
explanation: Adroit – दक्ष, चतुर। Potent – मशक्त। Skilled – दक्ष, चतुर। Powerful – शक्तिशाली। So, the synonym of 'Adroit' is Skilled.  /  Adroit means dexterous/skilled. Its synonym is 'Skilled'.

question: Which alternative is the correct indirect form of the sentence? He said to me, "When will you come back?"  /  Which alternative is the correct indirect form: He said to me, "When will you come back?"

options.A: He asked me when he would go back.
options.B: He asked me when I would go back.
options.C: He asked me when you would go back.
options.D: He asked to me when I would go back

explanation: Direct sentence में Reported speech question word से Start है। Indirect sentence बनाने के नियम: 1. Reporting verb के 'said to' को Reported speech के sense के अनुसार 'asked' में परिवर्तन। 2. Question word 'When' का प्रयोग। 3. Pronoun 'You' का परिवर्तन Reporting verb के object 'me' के अनुसार 'I' होगा।  /  Indirect: 'He asked me when I would go back.' Rules: said to→asked, you→I, will→would.

question: Change the following sentence into Active voice: The bird was killed by a cruel boy.  /  Change into Active voice: The bird was killed by a cruel boy.

options.A: A cruel boy was killed the bird.
options.B: A boy killed the cruel bird.
options.C: A cruel boy had killed the bird.
options.D: A cruel boy killed the bird.

explanation: P.V. → Object + was/were + V IIIrd + by + subject. A.V. → Subject + V IInd + object. अतः सही वाक्य: A cruel boy killed the bird.  /  Active voice: 'A cruel boy killed the bird.' (Subject + V2 + Object)

question: Change the following sentence into Passive voice: He kept me waiting.  /  Change into Passive voice: He kept me waiting.

options.A: I was kept waiting by him.
options.C: I had been waited by him

explanation: Active voice का Sentence past indefinite tense का है अतः Passive voice में बदलने का नियम: A.V. → Subject + V IInd + object; P.V. → Object + was/were + V IIIrd + by + subject. अतः सही वाक्य: I was kept waiting by him.  /  Passive: 'I was kept waiting by him.' (Object + was/were + V3 + by + Subject)

question: Which of the following sentences has a 'model verb'?
explanation: Model Verb– Model verb वह verb होते हैं जिससे अनेक भाव likelihood, ability, obligation या order प्राप्त होते हैं, जैसे: can, could, must, might etc. So, the correct sentence is: You must improve your spelling.  /  'Must' is a modal verb. Option (b) contains the modal verb 'must'.

question: Fill in the blank in the following sentences with the correct word from the alternatives: He solved all the questions with ..........  /  Fill in the blank: He solved all the questions with ..........
explanation: Preposition 'with' के बाद noun words आते हैं अतः ease– आराम का प्रयोग होगा। अन्य शब्द Appropriate नहीं हैं। Correct sentence: He solved all the questions with ease.  /  'With' is followed by a noun, so 'ease' (noun) is correct: 'He solved all the questions with ease.'

question: Fill in the blank with the correct form of pronoun in the following sentence: Rama and ............... were present.  /  Fill in the blank with the correct pronoun: Rama and ............... were present.
explanation: Rama and ........... दिये गये वाक्य में subject part है, अतः रिक्त स्थान में subject 'I' का ही प्रयोग होगा। Me, I का object form होता है, अतः गलत है। So, the correct sentence: Rama and I were present.  /  'I' is the subject pronoun. Correct: 'Rama and I were present.'

question: Which part of speech is the word 'ingenious'?
explanation: Ingenious – प्रवीण, जो कि Adjective का word है। Adjective – An adjective is a word used to quality a noun or a pronoun. विशेषण किसी noun या pronoun की विशेषता बताता है।  /  'Ingenious' means clever/skillful – it is an Adjective.

question: The feminine gender of the word 'Bachelor' is
explanation: Bachelor – कुँआरा लड़का का feminine word Spinster – कुँआरी लड़की है। Feminine Gender – वह संज्ञा शब्द जिससे मादा जाति (female) का बोध हो, Feminine gender word कहलाती है। Wizard – जादूगर, Steward – प्रबंधक, Woman – महिला।  /  Feminine of 'Bachelor' (unmarried man) is 'Spinster' (unmarried woman).

question: Change the following sentence into a negative one: This boy plays football.  /  Change into negative: This boy plays football.

options.A: This boy does not play football.
options.D: This boy do not plays football.

explanation: दिया गया वाक्य Present indefinite tense का है, अतः इसको Negative sentence में निम्नलिखित Rule के अनुसार change करेंगे: Sub + do/does + not + V Ist + Object. So, the correct sentence: This boy does not play football.  /  Negative of Present simple: Sub + do/does + not + V1 + Object = 'This boy does not play football.'

question: Change the following sentence into a question: Madhusudan knows how to cheat.  /  Change into a question: Madhusudan knows how to cheat.

options.A: Do Madhusudan knows how to cheat?
options.B: What Madhusudan knows how to cheat?
options.C: Does Madhusudan knows how to cheat?
options.D: Does Madhusudan know how to cheat?

explanation: दिया गया वाक्य Present Indefinite tense का है, अतः Interrogative sentence में बदलने का नियम: 1. do/does + subject + V Ist + object. 2. why + do/does + subject + V Ist + object. So, the correct sentence: Does Madhusudan know how to cheat?  /  Interrogative of Present simple: Does + Subject + V1? = 'Does Madhusudan know how to cheat?'

question: Which suffix should be used to pluralize the following word? taxi  /  Which suffix should be used to pluralize 'taxi'?
explanation: Taxi शब्द को s जोड़कर plural बनाया जाता है। जैसे: taxis. Suffix (प्रत्यय) – शब्द के अंत में लगने वाला शब्द या अक्षर, जिससे शब्द का अर्थ या प्रकार बदल जाता है।  /  'Taxi' is pluralized by adding '-s': taxis.

question: What is the meaning of the word 'utter'?
explanation: दिये गये Stanza के अनुसार 'utter' means 'speak' होगा।  /  'Utter' means 'Speak' according to the given stanza.

question: What is the mood of the poet? (Stanza-based)
explanation: दिये गये Stanza में cold और gray जैसे शब्दों का इस्तेमाल हुआ है जो poet के mood को pessimistic reflect करता है। Pessimistic – निराशावादी।  /  The poet's mood is Pessimistic, as indicated by words like 'cold' and 'gray' in the stanza.

question: Which one of the following has the opposite meaning to the word 'frustrater' in the passage?
explanation: दिये गये passage के अनुसार 'frustrater' – हतोत्साहित का Appropriate opposite word 'promoter' – समर्थक या प्रोत्साहक होगा। Executor – निष्पादक, Applauder – सराहना करने वाला, Approve – अनुमोदित करना।  /  Opposite of 'frustrater' (one who frustrates/discourages) is 'Promoter' (one who encourages).

question: The word 'drastic' in the passage means
explanation: दिये गये passage के भाव के अनुसार, Drastic (प्रबल) का correct meaningful word Determined (दृढ़-संकल्प) होगा। Powerful – शक्तिशाली, Consistent – संगत, तर्कयुक्त, Orderly – क्रमानुसार, सुव्यवस्थित।  /  'Drastic' in the passage means 'Determined' (resolute/forceful).

question: Fill in the blanks with correct preposition: Napoleon had genius ............... military tactics, without doubt he is genius ........... Mathematics.  /  Fill in the blanks: Napoleon had genius ... military tactics, he is genius ... Mathematics.
explanation: दिये गये रिक्त स्थानों में 'genius' के साथ 'military tactics' के सम्बन्ध में 'for' व genius के साथ किसी subject (विषय) में पारंगत के लिए 'in' का प्रयोग करते हैं। Correct sentence: Napoleon had genius for military tactics, without doubt he is genius in mathematics.  /  'Genius for' (military tactics) and 'genius in' (mathematics/subject). Correct: 'Napoleon had genius for military tactics... genius in Mathematics.'

question: Complete the following sentence with the most appropriate word: Priya is not ........... for this kind of a job.  /  Complete: Priya is not ........... for this kind of a job.
explanation: Cut out – छोड़ना, वंचित करना। Cut up – दुःखित होना। Cut through – तय करना। Cut in – बीच में टोकना। So, the correct sentence is: Priya is not cut out for this kind of a job.  /  'Cut out for' means suited/fit for. Correct: 'Priya is not cut out for this kind of a job.'

question: Which of the following nouns is a material noun?
explanation: Material noun (द्रव्यवाचक संज्ञा) – जिस Noun (संज्ञा) से ऐसे पदार्थ का बोध हो जिससे दूसरी वस्तुएँ बन सके उसे material noun कहते हैं। जैसे: Silver, Iron word इत्यादि।  /  Iron is a Material Noun – a substance from which other things can be made.

question: Which of the following is a grammatically acceptable sentence?

options.A: Give me four fives rupees notes.
options.B: Give me four five rupees note.
options.C: Give me four five rupee notes.
options.D: Give me four five rupees notes.

explanation: जब किसी वाक्य में Rupee और Note की संख्या को प्रदर्शित करना हो तो Note को हमेशा plural form में लिखते हैं वजाय Rupee के। So, the correct sentence: Give me four five rupee notes.  /  Correct: 'Give me four five rupee notes.' Note is plural, Rupee stays singular as an adjective.

question: Identify the subject of the verb in the following sentence: Every boy along with his parents is waiting in the next room.  /  Identify the subject of the verb: Every boy along with his parents is waiting in the next room.

options.C: Every boy along with his parents

explanation: Subject Part – किसी भी वाक्य में Auxiliary verb से पहले प्रयुक्त किये गये शब्द या वाक्य को Subject part कहते हैं। अतः Subject part of given sentence is: Every boy along with his parents.  /  Subject of the sentence is 'Every boy along with his parents' – everything before the auxiliary verb.

question: Which of the following sentence has an adjective clause?

options.B: I bought a chain which was beautiful
options.C: I bought a chain because it was beautiful
options.D: I bought a chain and it was beautiful

explanation: दिये गये options में option (b) में adjective clause का प्रयोग किया गया है। इसमें 'which' का प्रयोग chain की विशेषता के लिए पूरे Clause के रूप में हुआ है। Adjective Clause – Adjective clauses modify nouns and usually begin with a relative pronoun.  /  Option (b) has an adjective clause 'which was beautiful' – modifies the noun 'chain' using a relative pronoun.

question: Which of the following sentence is in the Passive Voice?

options.A: He was made to surrender his passport.
options.B: They feed the lions in the zoo twice.
options.D: We will not admit children under sixteen.

explanation: Option (a) is in Passive Voice. Helping verb 'was' is followed by V₃; this condition applies only in passive voice.  /  Option (a) 'He was made to surrender his passport' is in Passive Voice (was + V₃).

question: Tick the figure of speech in the following sentence: Cocks crow cock a doodle do.  /  Tick the figure of speech: Cocks crow cock a doodle do.
explanation: Onomatopoeia — words that imitate the natural sounds of a thing, making descriptions more expressive and interesting.  /  Onomatopoeia — 'cock a doodle do' imitates the natural sound of a cock crowing.

question: Point out the correctly spelt word
explanation: Correct spelling is Hemorrhage. Hence option (b) would be correct.  /  Correct spelling is 'Hemorrhage'.

question: Point out the meaning of the following proverb: Every cloud has a silver lining.  /  Point out the meaning: Every cloud has a silver lining.

options.A: Clouds are never without a silver lining.
options.B: There is a positive side to every unpleasant situation.
options.C: There is no hope in an unpleasant situation.
options.D: Clouds always have white lines in them.

explanation: There is a positive side to every unpleasant situation.  /  'Every cloud has a silver lining' means there is a positive side to every unpleasant situation.

question: Point out the word which can be substituted for the given sentence: The act of killing one's wife.  /  One word substitution: The act of killing one's wife.
explanation: Uxoricide means the act of killing one's wife. Hence option (b) would be correct.  /  Uxoricide = the act of killing one's wife.

question: Select the Adverb of place.
explanation: 'Here' is an Adverb of place. Agra and Japan are Proper Nouns. Almost is an adverb of completeness.  /  'Here' is the Adverb of place. Agra and Japan are proper nouns; Almost is an adverb of degree.

question: Which of the following combination is found in the structure of English language?
explanation: Subject + verb + object is the basic structure of English language.  /  Subject-Verb-Object (SVO) is the basic structure of the English language.

question: Point out the portion having a mistake: 'The poors are always helped by them who are kind and generous'  /  Point out the portion having a mistake: 'The poors(A) are always helped(B) by them(C) who are kind and generous(D)'

options.D: D  /  D (who are kind and generous)

explanation: 'The poor' means poor people; we should use only 'poor', not 'poors'. Correct: The poor are always helped by them who are kind and generous.  /  'Poors' is incorrect; it should be 'poor' (uncountable). Correct: 'The poor are always helped...'

question: Feminine of 'Hart' is
explanation: The Feminine of 'Hart' is 'Roe'. Hence option (c) would be correct.  /  The feminine of 'Hart' (male deer) is 'Roe' (female deer).

question: '______ late, he is a skilled worker.' Fill in the blank
explanation: 'Though' is suitable here. Correct sentence: Though late, he is a skilled worker.  /  Correct: 'Though late, he is a skilled worker.' 'Though' introduces a concession.

question: 'Call the first witness' said the judge. Convert to reported speech.

options.A: The judge commanded them to call the first witness.
options.B: The judge asked them to call witness first.
options.C: The judge told them to call first the witness.
options.D: The judge asked them to invite the first witness.

explanation: The judge commanded them to call the first witness. Imperative sentences in direct speech use command/request verbs in indirect speech.  /  Reported speech of imperative: 'The judge commanded them to call the first witness.'

question: Give one word substitution for the following: A hater of women.  /  Give one word substitution: A hater of women.
explanation: A hater of women is called Misogynist. Hence option (b) would be correct.  /  Misogynist = a person who hates women.

question: After reading a story on fish, if a teacher asks children to answer – 'Imagine you are fish in a pond. What do you see around you?' This is an example of  /  After reading a story on fish, 'Imagine you are a fish in a pond. What do you see around you?' This is an example of
explanation: Option (d) is correct. An open-ended question is designed to encourage a full, meaningful answer using the subject's own knowledge and/or feelings.  /  This is an Open-ended question — it encourages creative, meaningful responses using the student's own knowledge.

question: 'Prediction' as a subskill is associated with
explanation: 'Prediction' is a subskill associated with reading (a strategy for reading and science learning).  /  'Prediction' is a reading subskill — predicting what comes next is a reading comprehension strategy.

question: Which method is known as natural method?
explanation: Direct method is known as natural method in teaching learning process. It teaches a foreign language using only that language, without emphasis on grammar.  /  Direct method is known as the Natural method — it teaches language using only the target language.

question: 'Operation Black Board' is for
explanation: Operation Black Board is for Primary schools. It was launched in 1987 to provide minimum essential facilities to all primary schools in the country.  /  Operation Black Board is for Primary schools, launched in 1987 under NPE-POA to provide minimum facilities.

question: Dipthongs are known as
explanation: Dipthongs are known as pure vowels. A sound formed by the combination of two vowels in a single syllable — it begins as one vowel and moves towards another.  /  Diphthongs are known as pure vowels — sounds formed by combining two vowel sounds in a single syllable.

question: The change taking place in our climate have become: (Based on passage about climate change)  /  The change taking place in our climate have become:

options.A: a matter of celebration for the scientists.  /  a matter of celebration for scientists
options.B: a matter of worry for the scientists.  /  a matter of worry for scientists
options.C: something that does not have any serious implication.  /  something without serious implication
options.D: something to be whiled away by the scientists.  /  something to be whiled away

explanation: The first line of the passage clearly suggests that the change taking place in our climate has become a matter of worry for scientists.  /  The passage states climatic changes have become a matter of worry for scientists.

question: The 'antonym' of the underlined word 'increase' is (from climate change passage)  /  The antonym of 'increase' is
explanation: Antonym of 'increase' is 'reduction' — the act of making something smaller in size, amount or number.  /  Antonym of 'increase' = 'reduction'.

question: The climatic changes taking place today are different from earlier changes as  /  The climatic changes today are different from earlier changes as

options.A: Today they are slower and more natural.
options.B: Today they are much faster and caused by the humans.  /  Today they are much faster and caused by humans.
options.C: Today they do not threaten the humans because of their speed.  /  Today they don't threaten humans.
options.D: Today men are affected by them easily.

explanation: After close study of this passage: Today they are much faster and caused by the humans.  /  Today's climatic changes are faster and caused by human activity.

question: Increase in global temperatures may result in
explanation: Increase in global temperatures may result in a long period without rains (droughts).  /  Rising global temperatures may result in prolonged droughts — long periods without rain.

question: The underlined word 'slower' in the passage is
explanation: 'Slower' is an adjective. An Adjective is a word that adds something about a noun or pronoun.  /  'Slower' is an Adjective — it modifies a noun.

question: Greenhouse gases refer to

options.A: emission of gases by the cars alone.  /  emission of gases by cars alone
options.B: emission of gases by the industries alone.  /  emission by industries alone
options.C: trapping of heat of the sun by the earth increasing temperatures alone.  /  trapping of heat alone
options.D: carbon dioxide being produced from any source  /  carbon dioxide from any source

explanation: Greenhouse gases refer to Carbon dioxide being produced from any source. The second para first line of this passage justifies the answer.  /  Greenhouse gases = carbon dioxide produced from any source (cars, industries, etc.).

question: The underlined word 'sinks' in the passage refers to

options.A: absorption of greenhouse gases by industries.  /  absorption by industries
options.B: reduction of greenhouse gases by industries.  /  reduction by industries
options.D: the plantation for absorbing harmful gases.  /  plantation for absorbing harmful gases

explanation: 'Sinks' is used in the sense of the plantation for absorbing harmful gases.  /  'Sinks' in the passage refers to plantations that absorb harmful greenhouse gases.

question: Who plays the part of the fool in The Merchant of Venice?
explanation: 'Launcelot Gobbo' plays the role of fool in 'The Merchant of Venice' written by William Shakespeare.  /  Launcelot Gobbo plays the fool in Shakespeare's 'The Merchant of Venice'.

question: What form of poetry is 'La Belle Dame Sans Merci'?
explanation: 'La Belle Dame Sans Merci' is composed by the great romantic poet John Keats. It is written in Ballad form — a narrative poem set to music.  /  'La Belle Dame Sans Merci' by John Keats is written in Ballad form.

question: 'The Light of Asia' was divided into ______ cantos.
explanation: 'The Light of Asia' is written by Sir Edwin Arnold in eight cantos.  /  'The Light of Asia' by Sir Edwin Arnold is divided into eight cantos.

question: In which of Shakespeare's plays are the following lines: 'The quality of mercy is not strained. It droppeth as the gentle rain from heaven.'  /  In which Shakespeare play: 'The quality of mercy is not strained. It droppeth as the gentle rain from heaven.'
explanation: The line is from The Merchant of Venice (Act IV Scene I) spoken by Portia (Heroin of the play).  /  The lines are from 'The Merchant of Venice' (Act IV, Scene I), spoken by Portia.

question: Which of the following is a short musical poem expressing the poet's emotion?
explanation: Lyric is a short musical poem expressing the poet's emotion.  /  A Lyric is a short musical poem expressing the poet's personal emotions.

question: Tick the correct option to complete the sentence: The Prime Minister held ______.  /  Complete: The Prime Minister held ______.

options.B: a press conference at the airport.

explanation: On grammatical point of view option (b) is correct: The Prime Minister held a press conference at the airport.  /  Correct: 'The Prime Minister held a press conference at the airport.' (article 'the' required before 'airport')

question: In the given passage, what do you understand by the word 'hobo'?

options.C: Someone who does not have a job or a house and moves from one place to other
options.D: Someone who fights with everyone and does not sit quietly ever  /  Someone who fights with everyone

explanation: 'Hobo' means a person who travels from place to place looking for work, especially on farms. Hence option (c) is correct.  /  'Hobo' means a person who travels from place to place looking for work, especially on farms.

question: 'Scurvy' means

options.B: a disease resulting from a lack of vitamin C
options.C: an injury caused to the body from freezing cold  /  an injury from freezing cold

explanation: 'Scurvy' means a disease caused by a lack of vitamin C. Hence option (b) is correct.  /  Scurvy is a disease caused by deficiency of Vitamin C.

question: Identify the wrong collective noun.
explanation: The wrong collective noun is option (c) 'Sheeps'. The correct collective noun for sheep is 'A flock of sheep' or 'A herd of sheep'. 'Sheeps' is grammatically incorrect.  /  'Sheeps' is wrong. The correct collective noun is 'a flock of sheep' or 'a herd of sheep'.

question: Identify the correct sentence among the following.
explanation: In option (a), 'see' means to notice or become aware of someone or something. 'Please see my certificates' is the correct answer.  /  'Please see my certificates' is correct. 'See' means to notice/become aware of something.

question: Complete the following sentence with the most appropriate word: Let us not a confuse liberty ........... license.  /  Let us not a confuse liberty ........... license.
explanation: 'Confuse' क्रिया के साथ with preposition का प्रयोग किया जाता है। The verb 'confuse' is followed by preposition 'with'. Hence: Let us not confuse liberty with license.  /  The verb 'confuse' is followed by 'with'. Correct: 'Let us not confuse liberty with license.'

question: Which conjunction will be the most appropriate to join the following sentences? He lost the prize. He tried his best.  /  Which conjunction joins: 'He lost the prize. He tried his best.'
explanation: Option (a) 'though' is appropriate. Hence correct sentence: Though he tried his best but lost the prize.  /  'Though' is the correct conjunction: 'Though he tried his best, he lost the prize.'

question: Which part of the following sentence is predicate? 'On Saturday morning my friends and I play football in the park.'  /  Which part is predicate: 'On Saturday morning my friends and I play football in the park.'

options.D: On Saturday morning ........... I play football in the park.  /  On Saturday morning ... I play football in the park.

explanation: Play football in the park is predicate while my friends and I is subject. Hence option (c) is correct. Predicate – After the auxiliary verb, all sections of sentence is called predicate.  /  'Play football in the park' is the predicate. The predicate is everything after the subject.

question: What will be the feminine gender of 'wizard'?
explanation: The Feminine gender of 'wizard' is 'witch'. Hence option (b) is correct.  /  The feminine gender of 'wizard' is 'witch'.

question: The masculine gender of 'vixen' is
explanation: The masculine gender of 'vixen' is 'Fox'. Hence option (c) is correct.  /  The masculine gender of 'vixen' (female fox) is 'fox'.

question: Which of the following sentences has the correct punctuation marks?

options.A: The minister said, elections will be held in M.P., Bihar and Orissa.
options.B: The minister said "elections will be held in MP, Bihar and Orissa".
options.C: The minister said, "Elections will be held in MP, Bihar and Orissa."
options.D: The Minister said "Elections will be held in MP, Bihar and Orissa."

explanation: Option (c) has the correct punctuation. The minister said, "Elections will be held in MP, Bihar and Orissa."  /  Option (c) has correct punctuation: The minister said, "Elections will be held in MP, Bihar and Orissa."

question: Which among the following suffixes is not suitable for the root word 'prevent'?  /  Which suffix is NOT suitable for 'prevent'?
explanation: The suffixes ive, able, ing are suitable for 'Prevent' as: Preventive, Preventable, Preventing. While suffix 'ious' is not suitable for the word. Hence option (d) is correct.  /  Suffixes 'ive', 'able', 'ing' suit 'prevent' (preventive, preventable, preventing). 'ious' does not fit.

question: Identify the phrasal verb in the following sentence: 'Thieves broke into the blank in the middle of the night.'  /  Identify the phrasal verb: 'Thieves broke into the blank in the middle of the night.'
explanation: 'Broke into' is a phrasal verb which means to enter a building by force. Hence option (b) is correct.  /  'Broke into' is a phrasal verb meaning to forcefully enter a building.

question: Complete the following sentence with the most suitable option: 'I didn't know ........ in the party.'  /  Complete: 'I didn't know ........ in the party.'
explanation: Option (b) 'anybody' is suitable in the place of blank space. Hence correct sentence: 'I did not know anybody in the party.'  /  Correct: 'I did not know anybody in the party.' 'Anybody' is used in negative sentences.

question: Which of the following is not a homophonic pair?  /  Which of the following is NOT a homophonic pair?
explanation: Option (b) is not a homophonic pair. Kite-Kit are not homophones. Hence option (b) is correct. Homophonic – words with same pronunciation.  /  'Kite-Kit' are NOT homophones (different pronunciation). Options (a), (c), (d) are homophonic pairs.

question: Which of the following words doesn't have a silent letter in the pronunciation?  /  Which word doesn't have a silent letter?
explanation: The word 'Orange' does not have a silent letter in the pronunciation. Hence option (c) is correct.  /  'Orange' has no silent letter. Honest (h silent), Knock (k silent), Debt (b silent).

question: Which part of speech is the word 'who' in the following sentence? 'God helps those who help themselves.'  /  Which part of speech is 'who' in: 'God helps those who help themselves.'
explanation: In the sentence 'God helps those who helps themselves', 'who' is used as a relative pronoun. Hence option (d) is correct.  /  'Who' is a relative pronoun in this sentence.

question: Which part of speech is the word 'so' in the following sentence? 'Shruti wanted to work, so she went to her room.'  /  Which part of speech is 'so' in: 'Shruti wanted to work, so she went to her room.'
explanation: In the sentence 'Shruti wanted to work so she went to her home', word 'so' is used as a conjunction which joins two clauses. Hence option (d) is correct.  /  'So' is used as a conjunction joining two clauses.

question: Which of the following sentences has a 'modal verb'?

options.D: Her mother ought to know what she is doing in Mumbai.

explanation: Can, could, may, might, will, would, shall, should, must and ought to are called modal verbs. Option (d) has modal verb – ought to. Hence option (d) is correct.  /  'Ought to' is a modal verb. Option (d) contains the modal verb 'ought to'.

question: Which of the following sentences has a possessive pronoun?

options.B: Ramesh who is my grandson is reading a poem.
options.C: Her sister is known to me.

explanation: Possessive case also refers to a type of pronoun (mine, your, his, hers, its, ours, theirs) that indicates ownership. Option (d) has possessive pronoun 'ours'. Hence option (d) is correct.  /  'Ours' is a possessive pronoun. Option (d) contains the possessive pronoun 'ours'.

question: Which of the following sentences has a collective noun?

options.A: Cattle are grazing in the field.
options.B: He has a lot of milk.

explanation: A collective noun is the name of a number of persons or things taken together and spoken of as one whole, as crowd, army, fleet, cattle, flock, herd etc. Option (a) sentence has a collective noun – Cattle. Hence option (a) is correct.  /  'Cattle' is a collective noun in option (a).

question: Identify the sentence in passive voice.

options.C: He will be taken care of.

explanation: Option (c) is in passive voice. Passive voice structure of future indefinite tense: Sub + will/shall + be + V₃ form + by + object. This structure is followed by option (c). Hence option (c) is correct.  /  Option (c) is in passive voice (future indefinite passive structure).

question: Identify the sentence in active voice.

options.A: French is not taught in my college.
options.B: Their friends will be caught red handed.
options.C: I know nothing about western philosophy.
options.D: He was knocked down by a car.

explanation: Option (c) is in Active voice. Other options are in passive voice. Hence option (c) is correct.  /  Option (c) 'I know nothing about western philosophy' is in active voice.

question: Which of the following sentences is an exclamatory one?

options.D: How beautiful is the morning today!

explanation: A sentence that expresses strong feeling is called an exclamatory sentence with exclamation, emotions etc and sign of exclamation '!'. Here option (d) 'How beautiful is the morning today!' is an exclamatory sentence.  /  Option (d) is an exclamatory sentence as it expresses strong feeling and has an exclamation mark.

question: Which of the following words has a silent letter in it?
explanation: Option (a) is correct. In word 'calm', 'l' is silent.  /  In 'Calm', the letter 'l' is silent.

question: Which of the following sentences has a direct narration?

options.A: He has told me that his father was a police officer.
options.B: My mother tells us that her uncle was a salesman.
options.C: He said to me, "Do not cross the limit."
options.D: Shyam said to me that he was not at ease yesterday.

explanation: Option (c) is in direct narration. In direct narration there are two speech – one reporting verb and other reported speech. Reported speech always be in inverted comma. Hence option (c) is correct.  /  Option (c) has direct narration – the reported speech is in inverted commas.

question: Which of the following sentences has the antonym of 'narrow'?
explanation: In the sentence 'I admire his broad outlook', narrow means measuring a short distance from one side to another, especially in relation to length; opposite of the word narrow is broad or wide. Hence option (a) is correct.  /  Antonym of 'narrow' is 'broad'. Option (a) contains the word 'broad'.

question: According to the NCF 2005, learning is ........ and ........ in its character.
explanation: The National Curriculum Framework 2005 is one of four National Curriculum Framework works published in 1975, 1988, 2000 and 2005. According to NCF 2005, learning is active and social in its character. Hence option (b) is correct.  /  As per NCF 2005, learning is active and social in character.

question: What does motivation do in the process of learning?

options.A: It sharpens memory of the learner.
options.B: It differentiates new learning from old learning.
options.D: It creates interest for learning among young learners.

explanation: Motivation creates interest for learning among young learners in the process of learning. Hence option (d) is correct.  /  Motivation creates interest for learning among young learners.

question: Fill in the blanks with appropriate prepositions: Ashoka is worthy ........... remembrance.  /  Fill in the blank: Ashoka is worthy ........... remembrance.
explanation: The word 'worthy' is followed by preposition 'of'. Worthy of someone/something. Correct sentence: Ashoka is worthy of remembrance. Hence option (d) is correct.  /  'Worthy' is followed by 'of'. Correct: 'Ashoka is worthy of remembrance.'

question: What is the subject of the following sentence? 'No man can serve two masters.'  /  What is the subject of: 'No man can serve two masters.'
explanation: 'No man' is the subject in the following sentence. No man – subject; Can – modal auxiliary verb; Serve – main verb; Two masters – object. Hence option (d) is correct.  /  'No man' is the subject of the sentence.

question: After reading a story on fish, if a teacher asks children – 'Imagine you are a fish in a pond. What do you see around you?' This is an example of  /  After reading a story on fish, 'Imagine you are a fish in a pond. What do you see around you?' This is an example of
explanation: Open-ended question छात्रों को अपने ज्ञान और भावनाओं के आधार पर पूर्ण और अर्थपूर्ण उत्तर देने के लिए प्रोत्साहित करता है।  /  This is an Open-ended question — it encourages creative, meaningful responses using the student's own knowledge.

question: Diphthongs are known as
explanation: Diphthongs को pure vowels कहा जाता है। ये एक ही syllable में दो vowel ध्वनियों के संयोजन से बनी ध्वनियाँ हैं।  /  Diphthongs are known as pure vowels — sounds formed by combining two vowel sounds in a single syllable.

question: The change taking place in our climate have become: (Based on passage about climate change)  /  The change taking place in our climate have become: (Passage-based)

options.A: a matter of celebration for the scientists.  /  a matter of celebration for scientists
options.B: a matter of worry for the scientists.  /  a matter of worry for scientists
options.C: something that does not have any serious implication.  /  something without serious implication
options.D: something to be whiled away by the scientists.  /  something to be whiled away

explanation: गद्यांश की पहली पंक्ति स्पष्ट करती है कि जलवायु में हो रहे परिवर्तन वैज्ञानिकों के लिए चिंता का विषय बन गए हैं।  /  The passage states climatic changes have become a matter of worry for scientists.

question: The 'antonym' of the underlined word 'increase' is (from climate change passage)  /  The antonym of 'increase' is (from passage)
explanation: 'Increase' का विलोम 'reduction' है — किसी वस्तु को आकार, मात्रा या संख्या में छोटा करने का कार्य।  /  Antonym of 'increase' = 'reduction'.

question: The climatic changes taking place today are different from earlier changes as  /  The climatic changes today are different from earlier changes as (Passage-based)

options.A: Today they are slower and more natural.
options.B: Today they are much faster and caused by the humans.  /  Today they are much faster and caused by humans.
options.C: Today they do not threaten the humans because of their speed.  /  Today they don't threaten humans.
options.D: Today men are affected by them easily.

explanation: गद्यांश के अनुसार: आज के जलवायु परिवर्तन बहुत तेज हैं और मानव-जनित हैं।  /  Today's climatic changes are faster and caused by human activity.

question: Increase in global temperatures may result in  /  Increase in global temperatures may result in (Passage-based)
explanation: वैश्विक तापमान में वृद्धि से लम्बे समय तक वर्षा न होना (सूखा) हो सकता है।  /  Rising global temperatures may result in prolonged droughts — long periods without rain.

question: Greenhouse gases refer to  /  Greenhouse gases refer to (Passage-based)

options.A: emission of gases by the cars alone.  /  emission of gases by cars alone
options.B: emission of gases by the industries alone.  /  emission by industries alone
options.C: trapping of heat of the sun by the earth increasing temperatures alone.  /  trapping of heat alone
options.D: carbon dioxide being produced from any source  /  carbon dioxide from any source

explanation: Greenhouse gases का तात्पर्य किसी भी स्रोत से उत्पन्न कार्बन डाइऑक्साइड से है।  /  Greenhouse gases = carbon dioxide produced from any source (cars, industries, etc.).
